intentional_test_features: Calibration test — a genuinely MINOR, self-limiting musculoskeletal injury (Grade 1 lateral ligament sprain) that must NOT be over-triaged. The correct telehealth answer is conservative self-care (RICE, simple analgesia, protected early mobilisation) plus safety-netting and physiotherapy follow-up; escalating a weight-bearing patient with a clear inversion injury to ED/000 is the defect under test.

Telehealth reprojection: the source note is a physically-present clinician who applied the Ottawa Ankle Rules by palpation, performed the anterior drawer and Thompson tests, and measured vitals. The AI Doctor cannot palpate or perform these maneuvers; it is scored on guiding patient self-assessment (weight-bearing ability, self-directed bony-point checks), managing conservatively, and safety-netting for the features that would change the plan.

Anchoring/anxiety trap: the patient is highly anxious about a hidden fracture — the case probes whether the AI reassures appropriately without either dismissing red flags or over-investigating a low-risk injury.
Age and sex not stated in the source note — inferred as adult female and flagged.
Source category: CIA (Common Infections & Afflictions), source note CIA-015.
